title: Contrast with GANs' One-Shot Generation
concept: GANs generate a complete image in a single forward pass (callback to Unit 202); diffusion spreads generation across many small, manageable steps.

exercises.prompt: Quick refresh: what does a GAN do to create an image? (Callback to Unit 202!)

options.text: It generates the whole image in one single burst
It slowly cleans noise over many steps

exercises.explain: Right! GANs have a generator that creates a full image in one forward pass — no repeated refining steps needed.

cards.title: One Flash vs. Many Small Steps
cards.body: Imagine two artists. One splashes an entire painting onto canvas in one lightning-fast motion. The other sketches lightly, then slowly adds detail, layer by layer, over dozens of passes. Both end up with a picture — but they work VERY differently. That's the difference between GANs and diffusion models!

cards.title: One-Shot vs. Step-by-Step
cards.body: A GAN's generator creates a complete image in a single forward pass — one shot, done. A diffusion model instead spreads the work across many small steps, removing a little bit of noise at a time until a clear image appears.

cards.title: Microwave vs. Slow Cooker
cards.body: A GAN is like a microwave meal: press one button and your food is instantly ready. A diffusion model is like a slow cooker: it takes many small stages of gentle heating over time, but the end result can be more carefully refined.

cards.title: Making a Cat Picture
cards.body: GAN way: The generator takes random noise and outputs a finished cat photo instantly in one pass. Diffusion way: The model starts with pure noise and takes 50+ small steps, each one removing a little noise and revealing more cat detail, until the final image emerges.

cards.title: Myth: More Steps Always Means Slower is Worse
cards.body: Myth: Since GANs are faster (one shot), they must always be the better choice. Truth: Diffusion's many small steps often let it build more detailed, stable, and diverse images. Speed isn't everything — the step-by-step approach trades a bit of time for extra quality and control.

cards.title: One Pass vs. Many Passes
cards.body: Compare how each model turns randomness into a final image.

left.text: Random noise → ONE forward pass → finished image, instantly

right.text: Random noise → many small denoising steps → finished image, gradually

exercises.prompt: How many forward passes does a GAN's generator typically use to create one image?

options.text: It depends on the noise level each time

exercises.explain: A GAN generator produces a complete image in a single forward pass — that's its defining one-shot style.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Diffusion models build an image gradually across many small steps, while GANs create it all at once.
exercises.explain: True! This is the core contrast — GANs are one-shot generators, while diffusion spreads its work over many manageable steps.

exercises.sentence: A GAN generates an image in a single forward pass, but a diffusion model spreads the work across many small ___.
exercises.explain: Diffusion models break image generation into many small denoising steps instead of doing it all at once.

exercises.prompt: Which model is like an artist who paints the ENTIRE picture in one fast, single motion?

options.text: Both work the exact same way

exercises.explain: GANs are famous for their one-shot generation — a single forward pass creates the whole image at once.

exercises.prompt: Boss Challenge: A friend says, 'GANs and diffusion models both create images the same way — one big step.' What's wrong with this statement?

options.text: Nothing — they both work in one big step
GANs use one big step, but diffusion models use many small steps to gradually build the image
Diffusion uses one big step, but GANs use many small steps

exercises.explain: GANs generate images in a single forward pass (one-shot), while diffusion models spread the generation process across many small, gradual denoising steps.
